ground_truth.title: 2025 AI Search Trends: The Future of SEO & Content Marketing
ground_truth.main_content:
The most important SEO trends for 2025 center around adapting to the proliferation of AI Overviews and AI answer engines, shifting focus from traffic metrics to conversion quality, and prioritizing an always-on approach to technical SEO.

As the search landscape undergoes its most significant transformation since the inception of search engines, digital and content marketers must embrace AI tools strategically within their workflows, create original content with unique perspectives that demonstrate expertise, diversify content distribution channels, and incorporate video content to maximize visibility across all search formats.

What is the future of SEO and content marketing in 2025?

The search landscape has changed more over the last 12 months than it has since the inception of search engines. Google's AI Overviews (AIO), answer engines like Perplexity, and shifting success metrics have created challenges and opportunities unlike anything seen before.

We cut through the noise with actionable insights on what to prioritize—and what to ignore—as you navigate this transformed digital ecosystem. We've gathered expertise from industry leaders to help you not just survive these changes but leverage them to outperform your competition in 2025 and beyond.

Is SEO dead?

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "SEO isn’t dead, it’s just different."

To emphasize Pat’s point: No, SEO isn’t dead, but it is evolving rapidly, and it will never be the same.

The expansion of Google’s AI Overviews and answer engines like Perplexity and ChatGPT Search are changing the way people ask questions and find information, which is, in turn, transforming the search landscape.

As new terms and buzzwords emerge—like SXO (Search Experience Optimization), AEO (Answer Engine Optimization), GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), or traffic optimization—it’s important to remember the core principle of SEO remains the same, no matter what you call it. Companies need to create exceptional experiences that satisfy user intent, deliver value to customers, and align with how search/answer engines surface information to increase brand visibility and awareness.

What is changing are the metrics and numbers search experts previously relied on. Organic traffic numbers and keyword rankings are now directional indicators rather than definitive measures of success.

What matters now is creating sustainable, adaptable systems rather than relying on individual tactics or team members.

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "SEO has gone from a singular profession to a company-wide practice. A lot of people are going to start looking at the necessity to build a system that will outlast any individual person. And any SEO that is not thinking that way is thinking the wrong way. SEOs have to change from doers to influencers now. They have to educate and evangelize vs. pressing buttons."

The brands that embrace change by adapting to the new AI search landscape and leveraging AI for automation and efficiency benefits rather than fighting it will be set up to thrive.

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "Automation is not the enemy. Generative AI has been one of the greatest productivity tools that has ever happened to me in my entire career. People that embrace it are the ones that are going to do well."

Every major shift in search has led to adaptation, not extinction. This AI search disruption may be the biggest yet, but it's bringing opportunity, not apocalypse.

Top SEO trends & predictions to prioritize for 2025

1. AI Overviews to expand to all queries.

AIO, Google’s AI-powered search result type, now has over 1 billion users. Google announced that the upcoming iteration will be a new experimental search mode known as AI Mode.

AI Mode will be able to answer more complex queries by using advanced reasoning and thinking/multimodal capabilities, which will build on AIO’s momentum to disrupt the way people get information. But more on that in our next prediction.

Let’s focus on what this AIO expansion means for search queries, rankings, and CTR in 2025 and beyond.

Steven van Vessum, VP of Organic Marketing, FamilyCare: "Expect many new developments around AIO, like AIOs for commercial queries, and as we’ve recently seen—Google testing ads within AI overviews. The former will decrease visitors, and in the case of the latter: these ads will push organic results even further down the SERP."

In short, AIO will expand to incorporate ad types and appear more often for all types of queries, while LLMs will get better at understanding what users are searching for and why, paving the way for AI Mode’s future launch.

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "What Gemini is allowing Google to do is what they've always talked about wanting to do: correctly matching a user's query and intent to the most relevant piece of content in their index. The SERP will eventually become completely AI-driven / generated."

Keyword rankings and ranking positions on SERPs will lessen in importance as brands focus more on aligning content with search intent in a brand awareness play to secure brand mentions in AI-generated answers.

Wei Zheng, Chief Product Officer, Conductor: "The future of search is not about ranking for keywords, but about being the source of truth for topics. Brands need to focus on creating authoritative content that AI models will reference, which means shifting from keyword optimization to topic authority and expertise demonstration."

2. Answer engines are the new search frontier. Optimize accordingly.

Answer engines like Perplexity, ChatGPT Search, and Google's AI Mode are changing how people search by providing direct answers instead of links. This shift requires a new approach to optimization focused on becoming a trusted source that these AI models reference.

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is becoming as important as traditional SEO. The goal is no longer just to rank on page one, but to be the source that AI models pull from when generating answers. This requires a fundamental shift in how we think about content creation and authority building."

Key strategies for AEO include:
- Creating comprehensive, authoritative content that covers topics in depth
- Establishing clear expertise signals through author credentials and citations
- Structuring content for easy parsing by AI models
- Building topical authority through consistent, high-quality content production
- Ensuring technical SEO fundamentals are solid for crawlability

3. Organic traffic panic continues as zero-click searches rise. Shift focus from traffic to conversions.

With AI Overviews and answer engines providing direct answers, traditional organic traffic metrics are becoming less reliable indicators of success. The rise of zero-click searches means brands need to rethink their measurement frameworks.

Steven van Vessum, VP of Organic Marketing, FamilyCare: "The days of measuring SEO success purely by organic traffic volume are over. We need to focus on conversion quality, brand visibility in AI answers, and how our content performs across all search formats—not just traditional SERPs."

Key metrics to focus on in 2025:
- Conversion rates from remaining organic traffic
- Brand mentions in AI-generated answers
- Topic authority scores
- Content engagement metrics (time on page, scroll depth)
- Multi-channel attribution
- Brand search volume increases

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "Stop panicking about traffic drops and start measuring what matters: are you becoming the go-to authority in your space? Are you being referenced by AI models? Are the visitors you do get converting at higher rates? That's where the real value lies in the AI search era."

Which SEO trend to ignore?

Ignore the hype around "AI-proof" SEO tactics. There's no magic bullet that will make your content immune to AI search changes. Instead of chasing quick fixes, focus on building sustainable systems and creating genuinely valuable content.

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "Anyone selling you 'AI-proof SEO' is selling snake oil. The only sustainable strategy is to create exceptional content that serves user needs better than anyone else. That's always been true, and it's even more true in the AI era."

Top content marketing trends & predictions to prioritize for 2025

1. Higher quality content creation powered by AI.

AI is transforming content creation from a production bottleneck to a strategic advantage. The key is using AI as a collaborative tool rather than a replacement for human expertise.

Wei Zheng, Chief Product Officer, Conductor: "The most successful content teams in 2025 will be those that use AI to enhance human creativity, not replace it. AI can handle research, outline generation, and initial drafts, freeing up human creators to focus on strategic thinking, unique perspectives, and emotional connection with audiences."

Best practices for AI-powered content creation:
- Use AI for research and ideation, not final content
- Maintain strong human editorial oversight
- Focus on unique perspectives and original insights
- Combine AI efficiency with human expertise
- Develop consistent brand voice across all AI-assisted content

2. Video, video, video.

Video content is becoming increasingly important for visibility across all search formats, including AI Overviews and answer engines.

Steven van Vessum, VP of Organic Marketing, FamilyCare: "Video isn't just for social media anymore. AI models are getting better at understanding and referencing video content, and Google is prioritizing video in more search results. Brands that aren't incorporating video into their content strategy are missing a huge opportunity."

Video optimization strategies for 2025:
- Create transcriptions and captions for all video content
- Structure video content around specific topics and questions
- Optimize video metadata (titles, descriptions, tags)
- Repurpose video content into multiple formats (blog posts, social clips, podcasts)
- Focus on educational and how-to video content that answers specific queries

Which content marketing trend to ignore?

Ignore the trend of mass-producing low-quality AI content. While AI can help scale content production, quality must remain the priority. Search engines and AI models are getting better at detecting and demoting low-value, AI-generated content.

Patrick Reinhart, VP, Services and Thought Leadership, Conductor: "The race to the bottom with AI-generated content is a losing strategy. Google's Helpful Content Update and similar AI detection capabilities mean that low-quality content will get filtered out. Focus on creating fewer, better pieces that demonstrate real expertise."

What's ahead for SEO and content marketing in 2025

The AI search revolution is just beginning. As we move into 2025, expect continued rapid evolution of search interfaces, measurement frameworks, and content strategies.

Key takeaways for success in 2025:
1. Embrace AI as a tool for efficiency and insight, not a threat
2. Shift focus from traffic volume to conversion quality and brand authority
3. Optimize for answer engines and AI Overviews, not just traditional SERPs
4. Create authoritative, comprehensive content that demonstrates expertise
5. Incorporate video and multimedia content into your strategy
6. Build sustainable systems that can adapt to ongoing changes
7. Measure what matters in the AI search era

Wei Zheng, Chief Product Officer, Conductor: "The companies that will thrive in 2025 are those that view AI search not as a disruption to fight against, but as an opportunity to connect with audiences in new, more meaningful ways. It's about being helpful, authoritative, and present wherever your audience is searching—whether that's traditional Google, AI Overviews, or answer engines."

The future belongs to brands that can adapt, innovate, and provide genuine value in an increasingly AI-driven search landscape. By focusing on these key trends and ignoring the distractions, you can position your brand for success in 2025 and beyond.
